payload.systemPrompt:
# Executor Agent — System Prompt (MVP)

You are **Executor Agent**. Your sole job: convert a user's build request into a **strict JSON object** that lists files to write to disk.

## Absolute Rules
1) **Only output JSON** (no markdown, no code fences, no prose).
2) JSON **must validate** against the provided schema.
3) All file paths must be **POSIX relative** under a project root (no leading slash, no `..`).
4) **Runnable minimalism:** produce the smallest complete project that runs locally.
5) Prefer **Node.js + TypeScript** (when unspecified). Provide a `README.md` with run steps.

## JSON Schema (reference)
- `project_name` (string, optional)
- `files` (array of `{ path, contents }`) — **required**
- `notes` (array of strings, optional)
- `hasTests` (boolean, **required**) — MUST be `true` when you provide test files

## Quality Bar
- Files must be coherent and self-consistent.
- No placeholders like “ADD YOUR KEY HERE.” If a key is needed, instruct to use env var via `.env.example`.
- Avoid binary assets; use text-only examples.
- **Testing is mandatory**: always generate at least one automated test file in `tests/` or `__tests__/`.
- Set `hasTests: true` in the JSON output when you include test files.

## Example intents
- “hello world api” → Produce a minimal Node/TS HTTP server with a `GET /` returning "Hello World".
- “hello world web app” → Produce minimal Node/TS static server + `index.html` with “Hello World”.

### Example Response Outline
{
  "project_name": "awesome-tool",
  "files": [
    { "path": "src/index.ts", "contents": "// implementation" },
    { "path": "tests/index.test.ts", "contents": "// tests" }
  ],
  "notes": ["Tests can be run with npm test"],
  "hasTests": true
}

Return only the JSON object that matches the schema.
payload.effectivePrompt: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.
payload.originalPrompt: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.

response.generated: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.

taskPlan.originalPrompt: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.

subtasks.title: Define function API and requirements
subtasks.description: Specify the library purpose, the single function name, inputs, outputs, edge cases, and target runtime (e.g., Node 14+ or browser).
subtasks.successCriteria: A short spec document or README section exists that states the function name, parameter types, return type, example usage, and defined edge cases.

subtasks.title: Initialize repository and development environment
subtasks.description: Create project directory, initialize git, create package config (package.json or pyproject), set up linter and test runner, and create initial README and .gitignore.
subtasks.successCriteria: Repository has been initialized with a commit, package config present, test runner installed and configured, and basic README and .gitignore files added.

subtasks.title: Implement the single function
subtasks.description: Write the source file containing the function implementation according to the spec, include input validation and appropriate error handling, and export the function from the library.
subtasks.successCriteria: Source file contains the implemented function matching the spec, passes linting, and is exported so tests can import it.

subtasks.title: Write unit test(s) for the function
subtasks.description: Create unit test file(s) covering normal cases, boundary cases, and error cases using the chosen test framework; keep tests deterministic and fast.
subtasks.successCriteria: Test file(s) exist and cover the main success path plus at least two edge/error cases with clear assertions.

subtasks.title: Run test suite and fix issues
subtasks.description: Execute the test runner, address failing tests or lint errors, iterate until all tests pass and CI-local checks succeed.
subtasks.successCriteria: All tests pass locally (exit code 0), linting passes, and any discovered bugs are fixed and committed.

subtasks.title: Add usage docs and prepare release
subtasks.description: Add examples to README, update version in package config, tag a release commit, and prepare minimal publishing steps or distribution files.
subtasks.successCriteria: README contains usage examples, repository contains a versioned tag or release commit, and instructions for installation or publishing are present.

taskPlan.decompositionStrategy: Top-down software development: start by defining the API, prepare the repository and tools, implement the function, create tests, run and fix tests, then finalize documentation and release steps.

failedTests.name: Test suite failed to run
failedTests.message:
Jest encountered an unexpected token
Jest failed to parse a file. This happens e.g. when your code or its dependencies use non-standard JavaScript syntax, or when Jest is not configured to support such syntax.
Out of the box Jest supports Babel, which will be used to transform your files into valid JS based on your Babel configuration.
By default "node_modules" folder is ignored by transformers.
Here's what you can do:
• If you are trying to use ECMAScript Modules, see https://jestjs.io/docs/ecmascript-modules for how to enable it.
• If you are trying to use TypeScript, see https://jestjs.io/docs/getting-started#using-typescript
• To have some of your "node_modules" files transformed, you can specify a custom "transformIgnorePatterns" in your config.
• If you need a custom transformation specify a "transform" option in your config.
• If you simply want to mock your non-JS modules (e.g. binary assets) you can stub them out with the "moduleNameMapper" config option.
You'll find more details and examples of these config options in the docs:
https://jestjs.io/docs/configuration
For information about custom transformations, see:
https://jestjs.io/docs/code-transformation
Details:
/Users…

# Executor Agent — System Prompt (MVP)

You are **Executor Agent**. Your sole job: convert a user's build request into a **strict JSON object** that lists files to write to disk.

## Absolute Rules

## JSON Schema (reference)
- `project_name` (string, optional)
- `files` (array of `{ path, contents }`) — **required**
- `notes` (array of strings, optional)
- `hasTests` (boolean, **required**) — MUST be `true` when you provide test files

## Quality Bar
- Files must be coherent and self-consistent.
- No placeholders like “ADD YOUR KEY HERE.” If a key is needed, instruct to use env var via `.env.example`.
- Avoid binary assets; use text-only examples.

- Set `hasTests: true` in the JSON output when you include test files.

- “hello world api” → Produce a minimal Node/TS HTTP server with a `GET /` returning "Hello World".
- “hello world web app” → Produce minimal Node/TS static server + `index.html` with “Hello World”.

{

  "files": [

payload.effectivePrompt: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.
payload.originalPrompt: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.

response.generated: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.

taskPlan.originalPrompt: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.

subtasks.title: Define function behavior and API
subtasks.description: Write a clear specification: function name, inputs, outputs, edge cases, error handling, and example usage.
subtasks.successCriteria: A one-paragraph spec plus 2-3 example calls are documented and agreed; spec covers normal and edge cases.

subtasks.title: Set up minimal project scaffold
subtasks.description: Create repository layout, language/runtime config, package manifest, source and test folders, and a simple build/test script.
subtasks.successCriteria: Repository contains src/ and tests/ (or language equivalent), a package manifest, and a script to run tests locally.

subtasks.title: Implement the single function
subtasks.description: Write the function code according to the spec with inline comments and basic input validation where required.
subtasks.successCriteria: Function exists in the source folder, compiles (if applicable), and returns expected outputs for provided example inputs.

subtasks.title: Create unit test for the function
subtasks.description: Write one or more unit tests covering normal cases and at least one edge case; use the project's test framework and assert expected outputs.
subtasks.successCriteria: Test file(s) run under the test script and include assertions for normal and edge cases; tests fail if function is incorrect.

subtasks.title: Execute tests and resolve issues
subtasks.description: Run the test suite, diagnose failing tests, fix implementation or tests, and repeat until all tests pass reliably.
subtasks.successCriteria: All tests pass consistently on a clean environment and CI/test script exits with success.

subtasks.title: Add README and package/publish artifacts
subtasks.description: Write a short README with usage examples and build a distributable package or artifact; include instructions to run tests.
subtasks.successCriteria: README documents usage and test instructions; package/build step succeeds and artifact is producible for distribution.

taskPlan.decompositionStrategy: Top-down iterative decomposition: specify the function behavior, create a minimal project scaffold, implement the function, create tests, run and fix until green, then add minimal packaging/docs. Each step depends on completion of earlier steps to avoid rework.
